content: src/cli/ commands are pure functions of (CliContext, deps, args) -> Promise<{exitCode}>. The commander wiring in main.ts builds production deps and calls them; tests inject stubs and capture stdio via WriteSink interface. Keeps each command unit-testable without subprocess spawn.

name: command-level deps injection
description: Each command file exports a typed Deps interface with concrete production fallbacks (spawnRun ?? spawn, bridgeRunStream ?? bridge, reapRun ?? reap). Tests pass stubs at the boundary; production passes nothing and the live functions run. Mirrors the spawn-injection pattern from registry/canopy.ts (mx-369f32) and projects/clone.ts.

title: warren run uses bridge+broker+reap, not raw burrow stream
rationale: Sending CLI events through the same warren bridge as the HTTP UI means a CLI-driven run lands in warren.events with the same dedup-by-seq semantics. An operator can switch from CLI to UI mid-run without losing scrollback. Cost is one in-memory broker per CLI invocation; benefit is single code path for event durability.

content: register-agent CLI scopes to a single agent by Proxy-wrapping CanopyClient.listAgents() to return only the named summary, then passes that to refreshAgentRegistry. Reuses the per-agent error isolation (mx-5cc992) for free — schema/render failures land in skipped[0] with their stable code instead of duplicating registerOne logic.

doctor command is intentionally lean in Phase 11: env-var presence (WARREN_API_TOKEN, CANOPY_REPO_URL), canopy clone dir existence, projects root path, and BurrowClient.probe(). Phase 13 (warren-a29f) wires the bwrap probe and mirrors the same checks into /readyz. New checks should land as additional pure check functions returning {name, ok, message?, hint?} so the JSON output stays append-only.

CLI exit codes: 0 on success, 1 on operational failure (validation/burrow/canopy/runtime), 2 on usage error (empty arg). 'warren run' returns 0 on terminal-state succeeded and 1 on failed/cancelled. Operators wire these into CI/cron without a custom exit-code lookup table.

name: stdio capture for tests
description: Tests build a CliContext whose stdout/stderr are { write: (c) => arr.push(c) } sinks. After the command runs, tests parse JSON.parse(arr[i]) to assert structured output. Avoids snapshot-style stdout comparison, which is brittle to formatter churn. Plays nicely with biome's lineWidth-100 formatter.
Biome's noImplicitAnyLet flags 'let config;' inside a try/catch where the type would be inferred from a later assignment. tsc accepts it but biome's lint rule errors.
resolution: Annotate the let with the assigned function's return type: 'let config: ReturnType<typeof loadCanopyRegistryConfigFromEnv>;'. Less verbose than re-typing the interface and resilient to refactors.

content: withCliDb(env, fn) is the lifecycle helper that opens warren.db (resolved from WARREN_DB_PATH or WARREN_DATA_DIR/warren.db), builds Repos, runs fn, and closes the db on exit. register-agent / add-project / run all use it; 'serve' does NOT (bootServer owns its own db); 'doctor' does NOT (no db touch needed).

warren doctor (Phase 13) emits seven checks in this order: WARREN_API_TOKEN, CANOPY_REPO_URL, canopy_clone, canopy_clean, projects_root, bwrap, burrow_reachable. canopy_clean shells out to git status --porcelain in cwd=localDir; an empty stdout means clean (a dirty tree means the operator touched the cache, which warren resets on every refresh — mx-4afd87). bwrap probe runs 'bwrap --version'; the failure hint references the apt package name (bubblewrap), not the binary. Supersedes mx-a96e2a's Phase-11-lean stance.

warren doctor (Phase 13 + R-02) now emits eight checks in this order: WARREN_API_TOKEN, CANOPY_REPO_URL, canopy_clone, canopy_clean, projects_root, bwrap, warren_config, burrow_reachable. The CLI dispatch in src/cli/main.ts wraps the doctor action in withCliDb() so warren_config can enumerate projects.listAll(); a missing/empty DB still produces an informational ok=true.

Scenario 11 (doctor exit codes) MUST set WARREN_DB_PATH to a scratch path. Doctor wraps in withCliDb (warren-3151) so the warren_config check can walk projects; the default DB path is /data/warren.db which can't be created on macOS dev hosts, so doctor crashes before emitting any checks (debug: exit=1 checks=0). Use join(ctx.tmp, 'scenario-11', 'warren.db') so openDatabase creates the parent dir.

'warren run' accepts --provider/--model flags (src/cli/main.ts) and forwards them as providerOverride/modelOverride to spawnRun. runRun (src/cli/commands/run.ts) defaults deps.warrenConfigs to a fresh createWarrenConfigCache() so the CLI surfaces .warren/defaults.json provider/model the same way the HTTP server does — without this, CLI runs would silently skip per-project defaults.

warren init (warren-bd22) scaffolds .warren/triggers.yaml + .warren/defaults.json as the producer-side affordance for R-02. Two target modes: --cwd <path> (default process.cwd()) for operators editing a local checkout, and --project <id> which writes into the warren clone (projects.get(id).localPath). Schema is enforced at scaffold time via parseDefaultsConfig before writing. Refuses to overwrite either file with exit 2 (ValidationError). defaultRole resolution: explicit --default-role must match a registered agent; else auto-fill only when exactly one agent is registered; else omit. The triggers.yaml template ends with literal '[]' so yaml.load returns an empty list (parseTriggersConfig accepts both []/null/undefined as 'no triggers'). No git side-effects — operator commits + pushes.

resolveDbUrl (src/cli/context.ts, warren-e2ea) replaces resolveDbPath. Same precedence as server/config.ts: WARREN_DB_URL > sqliteUrlForPath(WARREN_DB_PATH) > sqliteUrlForPath(<DATA_DIR>/warren.db). Returns { url, conflict } where conflict carries the legacy WARREN_DB_PATH value when an explicit WARREN_DB_URL disagrees (sqliteUrlForPath(path) !== url). withCliDb opens via openDatabase({ url }) and narrows AnyWarrenDb to sqlite at the createRepos boundary — pg URL throws a clear 'CLI repo layer is sqlite-only today; see pl-f17e step 7' error. doctor command receives the live db handle from withCliDb so the db_reachable probe runs against the active sqlite handle.

warren doctor check order is now ten checks (R-13 step 5, warren-e2ea added warren_db + db_reachable): WARREN_API_TOKEN, CANOPY_REPO_URL, warren_db, db_reachable, canopy_clone, canopy_clean, projects_root, bwrap, warren_config, burrow_reachable. The db checks land immediately after the env-var section so a misconfigured WARREN_DB_URL fails ahead of the canopy/filesystem/burrow probes.

title: warren db subcommand group + migrate-to-postgres
rationale: R-13 (pl-f17e step 8, warren-14ac) lands as a 'db' subcommand group so future db-admin tools (dump, restore, prune) don't pollute warren's top-level help. First child is 'migrate-to-postgres' which copies an existing SQLite warren.db into a fresh Postgres database with INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING + a final setval on the events serial sequence. Per-table JSON summary on stdout {ok, tables:[{name,sourceRows,inserted,skipped}]}. FK-safe order: agents, projects, workers, burrows, runs, events, triggers. Source is opened with skipMigrations:true (read-only intent); target is opened with default migrations so a fresh empty pg DB is a valid --to target. Mismatched dialects (e.g. --from postgres://...) are usage errors (exit 2).

content: src/cli/commands/config-migrate.ts implements 'warren config migrate' (warren-5840): reads legacy .warren/defaults.json, splits preview into its own preview.yaml, writes the rest to config.yaml, deletes the legacy file. Refuses to clobber existing config.yaml or preview.yaml with a recovery hint pointing at hand-merge + retry. Malformed source aborts with the schema / parse error verbatim — never half-writes. Mirrors 'warren init' --cwd / --project targeting; no git side-effects (operator owns the commit).

listSeedStatuses (src/seeds-cli/list.ts, warren-6807) is the full-set sibling of listScheduledSeeds: same `sd list --format json` shell + SeedsListEnvelopeSchema parse, but returns ReadonlyMap<seedId,status> for EVERY issue (no scheduledFor filter). Used by GET /projects/:id/ready-plans to test open/closed children.

WarrenClient plan-run surface (warren-fcc8): cancelPlanRun(id) POSTs /plan-runs/:id/cancel returning CancelPlanRunResponse {planRun, cancelledChild, alreadyTerminal}; streamPlanRunEvents(id, {follow, signal}) tails /plan-runs/:id/events with NO sinceSeq (endpoint replays full union snapshot per (re)open — dedupe client-side by (runId, seq)); waitForPlanRun polls getPlanRun().planRun via isTerminalPlanRunState. Shared NDJSON reader extracted to src/client/ndjson.ts (readNdjsonStream + errorFromResponse) and reused by both stream methods + request error mapping. To stay under the frozen 569-line check:size budget, Plot methods moved to src/client/plots.ts free functions (class delegates via this as PlotRequester) and plan-run wire types to src/client/types.plan-runs.ts (re-exported from types.ts).

warren plan run/cancel (warren-ec6a, src/cli/commands/plan-run.ts) is the first CLI command family without withCliDb — it talks to a remote warren via WarrenClient.fromEnv(context.env). Flow mirrors warren run: probe first, POST /plan-runs, print {planRun,children} NDJSON summary, tail streamPlanRunEvents until terminal, map succeeded->0 else 1. --no-follow dispatches+exits 0. SIGINT detaches the tail (exit 130) without cancelling the remote run via an injectable onSigint/exit seam; second SIGINT force-exits. Output is NDJSON for pipeline parity (pretty renderer deferred to warren-ae0a).

name: plan-run output renderer abstraction
description: warren plan run/cancel select output via createRenderer(output, sink) (src/cli/plan-run-renderer.ts, warren-ae0a): a PlanRunRenderer interface (dispatched/event/terminal/cancelled) with an ndjson impl (pipeline parity, default) and a pretty impl porting run-plan.sh's jq filter — [HH:MM:SS] timestamped assistant/thinking/tool_use/tool_result/result blocks with whitespace-collapsing truncation, plus plan_run.* lifecycle one-liners. --output flag parsed by parsePlanRunOutput in main.ts, defaulting ndjson.

content: warren plan status <id> + plan list [--project --state] (warren-5e3f, src/cli/commands/plan-status.ts) round out the plan group: read-only one-shot GETs (no event tail) that probe-first then render. status joins child rows to fanned-out runs[] by runId for per-child cost (costUsd) + duration (endedAt-startedAt). Both default to NDJSON (one line per row) with opt-in --output pretty aligned tables. --state filter parsed via parsePlanRunState in main.ts.
